title: test: Fix the typo in bud test
body:
The command for get buildah inspect results from jq have typo in test "bud with no instructions but with CLI flags that require a new image be written". Fix it in this commit.

#### What type of PR is this?

/kind failing-test 

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Fix a typo in the bud.bats

#### How to verify it

Run test "bud with no instructions but with CLI flags that require a new image be written"

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```None

```

comments.body: Is this test failing in a particular environment?  It isn't failing in other pull requests that don't include this change, and the version that includes the "." looks more like the example in the "Object Identifier-Index: .foo, .foo.bar" section of the man page for jq, so I don't think I follow what's going on.

> Is this test failing in a particular environment? It isn't failing in other pull requests that don't include this change, and the version that includes the "." looks more like the example in the "Object Identifier-Index: .foo, .foo.bar" section of the man page for jq, so I don't think I follow what's going on.

Yes. This case failed in my test machine with RHEL:

> Is this test failing in a particular environment? It isn't failing in other pull requests that don't include this change, and the version that includes the "." looks more like the example in the "Object Identifier-Index: .foo, .foo.bar" section of the man page for jq, so I don't think I follow what's going on.

AFAICT that syntax is newer and likely not in the RHEL version? I remember fixing a similar syntax in aardvark-dns https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/pull/539/changes

But I wonder do you need the brackets at all, can this not just be `.OCIv1.config.Labels.A` as this should be a normal object key?

Yes. Currently the jq from RHEL 8 and RHEL 9 are both 1.6 and didn’t support the new syntax. The test works well in RHEL 10. To keep the tests can pass in RHEL8 and RHEL9 we should keep the old syntax for a while. Thanks a lot for your reviewing.
